name: basejump
description: Convert and copy numeric values between bases (hex, decimal, binary, octal)

markdownEnumDescriptions: **Copy to Clipboard** — Selecting a conversion copies the converted value to the clipboard.
**Replace in Editor** — Selecting a conversion replaces the source token in the editor with the converted value. _(default)_
**Insert Comment** — Selecting a conversion inserts a language-appropriate comment line above the token's line with the converted value.
basejump.defaultAction.markdownDescription:
The default action when selecting a conversion from the **Convert Number** quick-pick menu.

The per-item copy and replace buttons in the quick-pick let you override this for any individual conversion without changing this setting.

basejump.enableOctal.markdownDescription: Include octal in number detection and the **Convert Number** quick-pick. Octal is rarely used outside a few specialised domains (e.g. Unix file permissions) and clutters the quick-pick for most users. Disabling this setting removes octal from the quick-pick and automatic detection, but the explicit **Convert to Octal** command remains available regardless of this setting.

basejump.enableDelimitedVariants.markdownDescription:
When enabled, the **Convert Number** quick-pick shows delimited variants alongside plain conversions:

- **Binary (nibbles)** — groups of 4 bits separated by the language delimiter (e.g. `0b1010'1010`)
- **Hexadecimal (bytes)** — groups of 2 hex digits (e.g. `0xFF'AA`)
- **Decimal (thousands)** — groups of 3 decimal digits (e.g. `1'000'000`)

Disable this to keep the list shorter when you never use delimited forms. This also controls whether delimited variants appear in the **Convert Editor Content** target list.

markdownEnumDescriptions:
**Apostrophe** `'` — The official digit separator in C++ (since C++14), Swift, Ruby, and several other languages. Ignored by the compiler.

Example: `0b1011'0101`  |  `0xFF'AA'BB`

**Underscore** `_` — Highly popular in programming languages (Python, Java, Rust) because the compiler ignores them, keeping code readable and functional.

Example: `0b1011_0101`  |  `0xFF_AA_BB`

**Space** — The most common and 'cleanest' way to separate nibbles in documentation or code comments.

Example: `0b1011 0101`

**Hyphen** `-` — Sometimes seen in hardware manuals or instructional materials to clearly segment the high and low nibbles.

Example: `0b1011-0101`

**Period** `.` — Occasionally used in networking or specific technical notations, though less common than the underscore in general programming.

Example: `0b1011.0101`
basejump.fallbackDelimiter.markdownDescription:
Fallback delimiter used to separate digit groups in Binary (nibbles), Hexadecimal (bytes), and Decimal (thousands) output. Only takes effect when no per-language override or built-in language default applies.

**Resolution order:**
1. Per-language override in your `settings.json` (e.g. `"[cpp]": { "basejump.fallbackDelimiter": "apostrophe" }`)
2. Built-in language defaults: C/C++ → `'`, Python/Rust/Java/Go/Kotlin/Swift → `_`, Markdown/plain text → space
3. This setting (global / workspace fallback)

markdownEnumDescriptions: **By Target** — Group conversions by the destination base (e.g., all conversions _to_ Hexadecimal together). _(default)_
**By Source** — Group conversions by the source base (e.g., all conversions _from_ Decimal together).
basejump.conversionGrouping.markdownDescription: Controls how conversion options are grouped in the **Convert Number** quick-pick menu. This setting only affects the quick-pick; direct conversion commands are not affected.

basejump.alwaysPrefixConversions.markdownDescription:
When enabled _(default)_, all conversions (quick-pick menu, direct commands, and **Convert Editor Content**) always produce a prefixed result (e.g. `0b1010`, `0xFF`, `0o17`).

When **disabled**, a prefix is only added to the result if the source token itself carried an explicit prefix (`0b`, `0x`, or `0o`). Tokens that were bare (e.g. `255`, `FF`, `1010`) will produce bare results (e.g. `FF`, `11111111`, `255`).

> **Note:** Disabling this increases ambiguity — the converted token may be valid in more than one base system, making it harder for tools (and future BaseJump conversions) to determine its intended base.

basejump.assumeBinaryWithoutPrefix.markdownDescription:
When enabled, an unprefixed token made entirely of `0` and `1` characters (with or without nibble delimiters, but no `0b` prefix) is treated as **Binary** and nothing else.

Takes precedence over _Assume Decimal Without Prefix_.

Example: with this enabled, `1010` is always converted as binary 1010 → `0xA`, whether using a direct command or the **Convert Number** quick-pick — rather than asking whether you meant binary 10, decimal 1010, or hexadecimal 4106.

basejump.assumeDecimalWithoutPrefix.markdownDescription:
When enabled, a numeric token with no explicit base prefix (`0b`/`0x`/`0o`) that consists only of decimal digits — or digit groups with thousands separators — is treated as **Decimal** and nothing else.

This prevents ambiguous literals like `255` from prompting a source-base picker.

Example: with this enabled, `255` is always converted as decimal 255 → `0xFF`, whether using a direct command or the **Convert Number** quick-pick — rather than asking whether you meant decimal 255 or hexadecimal 597.

markdownEnumDescriptions: **Submenu** — BaseJump commands appear under a single **BaseJump** submenu in the editor context menu. _(default)_
**Top Level** — BaseJump commands appear directly in the editor context menu, separated from other items by divider lines.
**None** — BaseJump does not appear in the editor context menu at all. Use the Command Palette or keyboard shortcuts instead.
basejump.contextMenuLayout.markdownDescription: Controls how BaseJump commands appear in the editor right-click context menu.

basejump.menuShowConvertNumber.markdownDescription: Show the **Convert Number** command in the editor context menu. This is the all-in-one option: a single menu entry that handles any conversion via a quick-pick. Prefer this for a minimal menu. Disable it and enable the explicit conversion commands below for finer-grained control over exactly which conversions appear in the menu.

basejump.menuShowConvertEditorContent.markdownDescription: Show the **Convert Editor Content** command in the editor context menu (both submenu and top-level layouts).

basejump.menuShowToggleDelimiters.markdownDescription: Show the **Toggle Delimiters** command in the editor context menu (both submenu and top-level layouts).

basejump.menuShowConvertToBinary.markdownDescription: Show the **Convert to Binary** command in the editor context menu (both submenu and top-level layouts).

basejump.menuShowConvertToBinaryDelimite.markdownDescription: Show the **Convert to Binary (nibbles)** command in the editor context menu (both submenu and top-level layouts).

basejump.menuShowConvertToOctal.markdownDescription: Show the **Convert to Octal** command in the editor context menu (both submenu and top-level layouts).

basejump.menuShowConvertToDecimal.markdownDescription: Show the **Convert to Decimal** command in the editor context menu (both submenu and top-level layouts).

basejump.menuShowConvertToDecimalDelimit.markdownDescription: Show the **Convert to Decimal (thousands)** command in the editor context menu (both submenu and top-level layouts).

basejump.menuShowConvertToHex.markdownDescription: Show the **Convert to Hexadecimal** command in the editor context menu (both submenu and top-level layouts).

basejump.menuShowConvertToHexDelimited.markdownDescription: Show the **Convert to Hexadecimal (bytes)** command in the editor context menu (both submenu and top-level layouts).

basejump.statusBarFavorites.markdownDescription: When enabled, each unique conversion target in your favorites list appears as a status bar button on the left side. If there are more than 3 unique targets, a single **$(zap) BaseJump** button is shown instead — clicking it opens a pop-up menu with all favorite targets listed. Clicking a button runs the equivalent **Convert To** command at the cursor. Manage favorites via the ★ button in the **Convert Number** quick-pick.

markdownEnumDescriptions: **Convert** — Replace the token in the editor with the converted value. _(default)_
**Copy to Clipboard** — Copy the converted value to the clipboard without modifying the editor.
**Insert Comment** — Insert a language-appropriate comment line above the token with the converted value.
basejump.statusBarClickAction.markdownDescription: The action performed when clicking a BaseJump status bar button (or selecting from the pop-up menu when more than 3 favorites are active). Has no effect when `basejump.statusBarFavorites` is disabled.

scripts.pretest: yarn run compile && yarn run lint
